     The Numbers Business Report

Terminator: Dark Fate (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $62,253,077Details
International Box Office $188,114,589
Worldwide Box Office $250,367,666
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $10,606,571
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $16,246,498
Total $26,853,069
Further financial details...

Synopsis

27 years after the events of Terminator 2: Judgment Day, a new, modified liquid metal Terminator is sent from the future by Skynet in order to terminate Dani Ramos, a hybrid cyborg human, and her friends. Sarah Connor comes to their aid, as well as the original Terminator, for a fight for the future.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$29,033,832 (46.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.14 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:24.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$185,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,086 opening theaters/4,086 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $81,282,397

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 1st, 2019 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
November 1st, 2019 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: October 25th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 25th, 2019 (Wide) (France)
October 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Thailand)
October 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Germany)
October 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Malaysia)
Video Release: January 28th, 2020 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for violence throughout, language and brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2600 (Cert #52317), 10/16/2019)
Running Time: 128 minutes
Franchise: Terminator
Cast, crew, or production detail: Archival Footage of Previous Installment, Intertitle, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Mexico, Mexico City
Plot point: Artificial Intelligence, Border Agents, Cyborg, Death of a Son or Daughter, Non-Chronological, Robot, Time Travel
Social setting: Adopted Family, Border Town
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Time period setting: 2020s, Near Future
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Paramount Pictures, Skydance Productions, Twentieth Century Fox, Tencent Pictures , TSG Entertainment, Lightstorm Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

27 years after the events of Terminator 2: Judgment Day, a new, modified liquid metal Terminator is sent from the future by Skynet in order to terminate Dani Ramos, a hybrid cyborg human, and her friends.
